title: FortuneWander
summary: Bespoke WordPress travel portal with SPA transitions and a custom AI Travel Consultant plugin powered by Google Gemini 2.5.
subtitle: Editorial Travel Portal & AI Itinerary Assistant
problem: The client needed a high-end editorial travel portal that didn't feel like a standard WordPress site, alongside an integrated AI assistant to generate complex travel itineraries automatically. Standard WordPress themes are monolithic, slow, and reload heavily on navigation.
goal: Engineer a cinematic, single-page application (SPA) experience on top of a monolithic CMS (WordPress) while building a secure, custom AI plugin that outputs rich, structured HTML without triggering native XSS filters.
solution: Integrated a modern Vite build pipeline, decoupled the frontend with Swup.js for AJAX routing, and engineered an object-oriented WordPress AI plugin using the Google Gemini 2.5 API with search grounding.

challenges.title: HTML Sanitization vs. Rich AI Itinerary Table Layouts
challenges.solution: Standard WordPress sanitization stripped the structural classes and table layouts returned by the AI. I developed a custom whitelist-based HTML sanitizer (`wp_kses` configuration) to explicitly permit UI tags and styling attributes, enabling rich visual reports while enforcing strict XSS security.

challenges.title: Decoupled WordPress Build & Asset Pipeline
challenges.solution: Bridging modern frontend build tools with WordPress required writing a custom PHP asset loader to dynamically parse Vite's build manifests and enqueue cache-busted modules, eliminating core render-blocking styles.

engineeringDecisions.decision: Custom REST API Endpoints with Nonce Auth
engineeringDecisions.reason: Needed a lightweight, secure way for the floating JS chat widget to communicate with the PHP backend without exposing the Gemini API key.
engineeringDecisions.tradeoff: Requires manual route registration and security hardening compared to using native AJAX, but provides a cleaner, RESTful architecture.

engineeringDecisions.reason: To prevent API quota exhaustion and abuse of the AI generator.
engineeringDecisions.tradeoff: IP-based limiting can block legitimate users on shared networks (like corporate offices), but it was the most efficient stateless solution.

results: Full SPA-like routing with 0 page reloads
Lightweight floating chat widget built with Vanilla JS
lessonsLearned: Integrating AJAX routing (Swup.js) into WordPress requires rigorous cleanup and re-binding of event listeners on every page load to prevent memory leaks.
LLMs generate unpredictable HTML; a robust, whitelist-based sanitization pipeline is critical for security.
Vite is vastly superior to Webpack for WordPress theme development due to its native ESM support.
futureImprovements: Implement user authentication to allow saving generated itineraries.
Transition from polling to WebSockets or SSE for the AI chat stream.
Add automated end-to-end (E2E) testing for the checkout flows.

title: QueenTrends Theme
summary: Minimalist editorial WordPress theme featuring Vite-powered asset bundling, GSAP animations, and AJAX page transitions.
subtitle: Digital Fashion Journalism Theme Architecture
problem: The client's existing WordPress theme was monolithic, heavily dependent on jQuery, and suffered from poor Core Web Vitals, causing low SEO rankings and a sluggish reading experience.
goal: Re-architect the theme from the ground up to achieve sub-second load times, eliminate jQuery, and provide an app-like navigation experience using SPA routing.
solution: Architected a hybrid theme using Vite HMR and Rollup hashing, component-based PHP partials, Swup.js container rehydration lifecycle hooks, and native transient-based security filters.

challenges.title: AJAX DOM Rehydration & Event Listeners
challenges.solution: Swup.js transitions caused broken event listeners and scroll positioning glitches. Solved by architecting a re-initialization pipeline bound to `swup.hooks.on('page:view')` to re-bind carousel instances and reset Lenis scroll coordinates asynchronously using `requestIdleCallback`.

challenges.title: Responsive Media Pipeline
challenges.solution: Created a custom PHP media rendering pipeline (`queentrends_responsive_image`) that dynamically constructs multi-density `srcset` arrays, enforces `loading='lazy'`, and provides deterministic fallback rendering to reduce payload sizes.

engineeringDecisions.reason: To reduce main-thread blocking time and improve Lighthouse scores.
engineeringDecisions.tradeoff: Required rewriting legacy plugins and custom carousels in Vanilla JS, increasing initial development time but significantly improving runtime performance.

engineeringDecisions.reason: To emulate modern frontend component architectures (like React) within standard PHP.
engineeringDecisions.tradeoff: Slightly increases server-side include overhead, but drastically improves code maintainability.

results: 80% reduction in image payloads via responsive srcset pipelines
lessonsLearned: WCAG-compliant focus management is difficult in 3D carousels; off-screen elements must be programmatically hidden from the tab sequence.
History API manipulation in WordPress requires strict attention to canonical URLs for SEO.
futureImprovements: Implement a Redis object cache for faster database queries.
Add automated visual regression testing using Playwright.

title: Zola
summary: Real-time collaborative sky & interactive constellation platform utilizing Supabase WebSockets and procedural graph generation.
subtitle: Real-Time Collaborative Constellation Engine
problem: Handling high-concurrency WebSocket data and rendering procedural graph animations (constellations) in real-time typically causes severe mobile GPU bottlenecks and layout thrashing.
goal: Build an ultra-responsive, 60fps real-time interactive canvas capable of procedurally generating organic constellation graphs without frame drops.
solution: Developed a custom Single-Hub Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) graph algorithm, backed by Supabase WebSockets and CSS GPU compositor acceleration with render-pausing logic.

challenges.title: Mobile GPU Frame Drops
challenges.solution: High-frequency star creation events triggered continuous React re-renders. Engineered an isolated `TierContext` state architecture that decoupled the background animations from main state updates and automatically paused background animation loops when interaction modals were active.

challenges.title: Graph Clutter & Structural Flickering
challenges.solution: Standard Delaunay triangulation caused tangled webs. Solved by engineering a custom Single-Hub Kruskal's MST algorithm applying deterministic string-hash jitter to distance calculations, ensuring stable constellation lines across re-renders.

engineeringDecisions.reason: Allowed for rapid deployment within a 48-hour timeframe while providing robust PostgreSQL persistence.
engineeringDecisions.tradeoff: Less control over the WebSocket payload optimization compared to a raw custom socket implementation.

engineeringDecisions.decision: SVG Overlays vs WebGL Canvas for Constellations
engineeringDecisions.reason: SVG provided easier declarative integration with React state and Framer Motion for line drawing animations.
engineeringDecisions.tradeoff: SVG DOM nodes become a bottleneck at >500 nodes; WebGL would scale better for massive graphs.

results: Deployed full-stack real-time application in 48 hours
lessonsLearned: Decoupling high-frequency data streams from React's main render tree is critical for performance.
Hardware-accelerated CSS transforms (`will-change: transform`) drastically improve animation smoothness but must be managed to avoid memory bloat.
futureImprovements: Migrate constellation rendering from SVG to WebGL/Three.js for infinite scaling.
Implement a Redis cache layer for initial payload hydration.

title: FinChat
summary: Localized RAG workspace for financial analysts, featuring dual lexical/semantic pipelines and cross-encoder reranking.
subtitle: Secure Financial RAG Pipeline
problem: Financial analysts need to query highly sensitive PDF reports, but cannot upload them to cloud APIs (like OpenAI) due to strict data privacy regulations. Furthermore, standard semantic search fails on exact numerical figures.
goal: Build a performant, zero-cost Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system that runs entirely locally on consumer hardware while accurately retrieving exact financial figures.
solution: Engineered an asynchronous FastAPI backend coordinating a dual-retrieval pipeline (FAISS + BM25) with a cross-encoder reranker, feeding context into a local `llama.cpp` instance for secure inference.

challenges.title: Numerical Search Inaccuracies in Semantic Retrieval
challenges.solution: Dense vector embeddings frequently fail to differentiate close numbers ('14.5%' vs '41.5%'). Implemented a hybrid pipeline merging FAISS scores with BM25Okapi keyword rankings, passed through a cross-encoder to retain precise numbers without losing semantic depth.

challenges.title: Page-Specific Citation Mapping
challenges.solution: Structured the text ingestion parser to capture absolute page indices and embed them in a custom chunk identifier. The frontend parses these citations into hash fragments (`#page=X`) to control an iframe PDF viewer.

engineeringDecisions.decision: Llama.cpp over HuggingFace Transformers for LLM inference
engineeringDecisions.reason: Llama.cpp provides significantly faster inference on CPU/consumer GPU via optimized C/C++ execution.
engineeringDecisions.tradeoff: Harder to set up and requires managing GGUF model files manually.

engineeringDecisions.reason: Required to achieve high precision for keyword/number lookups while retaining semantic understanding of queries.
engineeringDecisions.tradeoff: Increases index building time and memory overhead.

results: Cross-encoder reranking eliminated >90% of irrelevant context chunks
lessonsLearned: Chunking strategy (size and overlap) impacts RAG quality more than the underlying LLM size.
Asynchronous Python (FastAPI + Asyncio) is vital when managing heavy concurrent ML inference tasks.
futureImprovements: Implement vision-based parsing (OCR) to extract data from image-based tables in PDFs.
Dockerize the entire pipeline for one-click deployment.

title: PrismNet (KJP-66)
summary: Compressed RT-DETR model achieving deterministic latency and a 50% size reduction for Edge AI hardware.
subtitle: Vision Transformer Compression Engine
problem: Deploying state-of-the-art vision transformers on consumer Edge AI hardware results in severe CPU bottlenecks and memory fragmentation, failing latency requirements.
goal: Drastically compress a high-accuracy Vision Transformer (RT-DETR) to achieve ultra-low deterministic latency on an entry-level mobile GPU.
solution: Applied PyTorch L1 Unstructured Pruning (30% sparsity) combined with INT8 Quantization, shrinking the model footprint and natively compiling it into a C++ TensorRT FP16 engine.

challenges.title: CPU-Bound NMS Bottleneck
challenges.solution: Standard object detectors rely on Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS), a CPU-bound process. By utilizing an NMS-Free Transformer Architecture (RT-DETR), the entire pipeline stays locked on the GPU, achieving zero post-processing lag.

engineeringDecisions.reason: TensorRT applies hardware-specific kernel auto-tuning (layer fusion) that maximizes throughput on NVIDIA GPUs.
engineeringDecisions.tradeoff: Models compiled with TensorRT are hardware-locked and must be recompiled for different GPU architectures.
Slight degradation in detection precision (mAP), mitigated by careful calibration sets.

results: Achieved 47.49 FPS on an entry-level RTX 2050
lessonsLearned: Memory bandwidth, not compute (FLOPs), is usually the primary bottleneck in edge inference.
Python runtime overhead is massive; migrating to C++ execution engines is mandatory for real-time edge AI.
futureImprovements: Explore structured pruning for actual FLOP reduction rather than just sparsity.
Port the inference pipeline to C++ completely, removing the Python wrapper.
